name: Moanalua Valley Trail
tagline: A wild 10-mile valley-to-summit trek through stream crossings and native forest to the Ko'olau ridge.
parking: Free parking at the end of Moanalua Valley Road — the neighborhood gate is open during daylight hours
bestTime: Start early — this is a long day hike and you want to summit before afternoon clouds
talkStory: This valley was the cherished estate of King Kamehameha V (Prince Lot), and the trail passes stone ruins of his summer retreat deeper in the valley. The famous Douglas fir at the park entrance was a gift from the Pacific Northwest in the 1800s. Ancient Hawaiians cultivated extensive taro systems here — you'll spot old rock-walled lo'i terraces along the stream during the 12+ stream crossings. The Ko'olau summit at the end was once part of a trail network connecting leeward and windward Oahu. Full-day commitment: 10 miles, 2,600 ft gain, start by 7am.
